action.reasoning: Observation: Latest eval score 0.5505 (worse than best 0.5744). Local test shows poor results: room=60%, outdoor=30% success. The planner is getting worse. The best score was 0.5744 from iter 10 with the simpler planner. Analysis: The 'improved' planner is actually worse. The original iter 10 planner had 87.3% success and 360m distance. I need to go back to basics. The key issue: my current planner has too many competing heuristics. Let me write a clean, simple planner: 1) Pick highest utility neighbor, 2) If tie, pick unvisited, 3) If all utility=0, BFS to nearest high-utility node globally, 4) If no utility anywhere, visit all unvisited nodes systematically. Also need to reduce travel distance by avoiding unnecessary backtracking.
